channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Why one of the cities most dependent on the Colorado River now has water for sale
item.description: Once one of the most dependent on the Colorado River, San Diego now may have water to sell to states that are seeing their supplies from the shrinking river cut.
item.content:encoded:
Once one of the most dependent on the Colorado River, San Diego now may have water to sell to states that are seeing their supplies from the shrinking river cut.

(Image credit: Annika Hammerschlag)
item.dc:creator: Kirk Siegler

item.title: Iowa voters pick their nominees for competitive general elections
item.description: Democratic state Rep. Josh Turek will face Republican U.S. Rep. Ashley Hinson in the Senate race. For governor, Democrat state Auditor Rob Sand and Republican businessman Zach Lahn move on to November.
item.content:encoded:
Democratic state Rep. Josh Turek will face Republican U.S. Rep. Ashley Hinson in the Senate race. For governor, Democrat state Auditor Rob Sand and Republican businessman Zach Lahn move on to November.

(Image credit: Stephen Maturen)
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler

item.title: Supreme Court reinstates Republican-favored Alabama congressional districts
item.description: The ruling means that Alabama's 2026 midterm elections will feature six Republican-leaning districts and one Democratic-leaning one.
item.content:encoded:
The ruling means that Alabama's 2026 midterm elections will feature six Republican-leaning districts and one Democratic-leaning one.

(Image credit: Tasos Katopodis)
item.dc:creator: Grady Martin

item.title: With its return home, major plumbing issue on board USS Ford can be addressed
item.description: After nearly a year at sea, the USS Ford is finally home. That means the crew can take some well-earned leave, but it also means plumbing problems on the $13 billion carrier can be fixed.
item.content:encoded: After nearly a year at sea, the USS Ford is finally home. That means the crew can take some well-earned leave, but it also means plumbing problems on the $13 billion carrier can be fixed.
item.dc:creator: Steve Walsh

item.title: DOJ will pause $1.8 billion fund, per court order. And, key primaries to watch today
item.description: The DOJ says it will abide by a federal court order pausing its anti-weaponization fund. And, six states are holding primaries today. Here are the races to watch.
item.content:encoded:
The DOJ says it will abide by a federal court order pausing its anti-weaponization fund. And, six states are holding primaries today. Here are the races to watch.

(Image credit: Win McNamee)
item.dc:creator: Suzanne Nuyen

item.title: Pentagon policy illegally banned transgender troops from military, appeals court rules
item.description: A divided panel of appeals court judges has ruled that a Trump administration policy illegally banned transgender troops from military service.
item.content:encoded:
A divided panel of appeals court judges has ruled that a Trump administration policy illegally banned transgender troops from military service.

(Image credit: Ahn Young-joon)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: For veterans, a place where peace can take root
item.description: Iraq war veteran John Follmer leads vet volunteers who are rehabbing a neglected Japanese garden on the West LA Veterans Affairs Campus.
item.content:encoded:
Iraq war veteran John Follmer leads vet volunteers who are rehabbing a neglected Japanese garden on the West LA Veterans Affairs Campus.

(Image credit: Stella Kalinina for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Quil Lawrence

item.title: Trump vowed to revoke hundreds of citizenships. It's proving harder to do
item.description: President Trump's vow to revoke citizenship worries immigrant advocates, legal scholars and naturalized Americans — but so far it's proving harder to do than the rhetoric suggests.
item.content:encoded: President Trump's vow to revoke citizenship worries immigrant advocates, legal scholars and naturalized Americans — but so far it's proving harder to do than the rhetoric suggests.
item.dc:creator: Jaclyn Diaz

item.title: With its return home, major plumbing issue on board USS Ford can be addressed
item.description: After nearly a year at sea, the USS Ford is finally home. That means the crew can take some well-earned leave, but it also means plumbing problems on the $13 billion carrier can be fixed.
item.content:encoded: After nearly a year at sea, the USS Ford is finally home. That means the crew can take some well-earned leave, but it also means plumbing problems on the $13 billion carrier can be fixed.
item.dc:creator: Steve Walsh

item.title: In Oklahoma, unused land at prisons becomes a pollinator's paradise
item.description: Pollinator gardens help migrating birds and butterflies. One state on their flight path is planting habitats at its prisons.
item.content:encoded: Pollinator gardens help migrating birds and butterflies. One state on their flight path is planting habitats at its prisons.
item.dc:creator: Chloe Bennett-Steele
